## Tine 0.2.7

This patch release focuses on release-readiness fixes for execution log durability, MCP coverage, and the desktop MCP onboarding path.

### Highlights

- Preserves streamed execution output if a final persistence update fails, then re-buffers the drained chunks so a later flush can still write them.
- Adds regression coverage for late stream chunks, failed stream flushes, shutdown draining, large streaming output, cache restart cases, queued lifecycle behavior, and ignored e2e paths.
- Improves the desktop app MCP path: the title bar now shows `MCP :<port>` whenever the embedded server is ready, and clicking it copies the matching `tine-mcp --api-url http://127.0.0.1:<port>` command.
- Documents the DMG/MSI MCP setup clearly: the desktop app starts the local API, but VS Code, Cursor, Claude, and other MCP hosts still need the `pip install tine` wrapper so they have a stdio command to launch.
- Keeps version metadata aligned across Rust crates, the Python package, Tauri config, and release artifacts at `0.2.7`.

### Validation

- Rust workspace check passed.
- API unit tests passed.
- MCP Python tests passed.
- UI helper and lifecycle scenario tests passed.
- Full API e2e passed, including the previously ignored tests.
- Release workflow built and smoked wrapper binaries for macOS and Windows.
- Release workflow built and smoked desktop DMG/MSI bundles.

### Known Note

The desktop bundle does not yet ship a standalone MCP launcher. Users who install only the desktop app can use the local UI and API, but MCP host connection still requires installing the Python wrapper with `pip install tine`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tinelabs/tine/compare/v0.2.6...v0.2.7
